hook.text: Data is encrypted at rest and in transit. But what about during processing? Confidential computing protects data in use.

buildup.text: Secure enclaves (TEEs) isolate data during processing. Not even the cloud provider or OS can access it.

discovery.text: Intel SGX, AMD SEV, and ARM TrustZone provide hardware-level isolation. Cloud providers offer these as managed features.

twist.text: Confidential computing lets healthcare and finance use the cloud for sensitive data they couldn't process before.

climax.text: The Confidential Computing Consortium is standardizing the tech. Full lifecycle encryption is the new normal.

punchline.text: Encrypted while processing. Not even the cloud can see your data.

quiz.question: What does confidential computing protect against?
options: Unauthorized access to data while it's being actively processed in memory
Data breaches during file transfers between data centers
Physical theft of hard drives from data centers
